item.title: "One or two were like, 'No, they're not beating the …
item.description: ESPN's Brian Windhorst revealed on his Brian Windhorst & The Hoop Collective podcast (14-minute mark) that he spoke to head coaches, general managers and scouts Monday about New York's chances in the NBA Finals against the Western Conference as the Knicks were putting the finishing touches on their sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers. "One or two were like, 'No, they're not beating the West. Forget about it. The Cavs are a first-round Western Conference team,'" Windhorst said. "But for the most par

item.title: If the Finals reaches six games, fans would have to …
item.description: If the Finals reaches six games, fans would have to fork up over $4,500 on June 16. It’s a stark contrast to 10 years ago when Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors faced off in the 2016 NBA Finals and the average price to get into those games was $880. Last year, when the Thunder and Pacers clashed, the average ticket price was $1,147, according to TickPick.

item.title: "(The Cavaliers) will enter the offseason as a second …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "(The Cavaliers) will enter the offseason as a second apron team again. So what does that mean? You've got to figure out, is it better off with James Harden on our roster moving for next year or James Harden off our roster and really, having Dennis Schroeder start, having a sign a guy to the vet minimum here, you get about $30 million in financial savings here, or are you going to do a new deal with Harden? You would think when you make the trade with Harden, he basically had to wai

item.title: Kenny Atkinson to remain as Cavaliers head coach
item.description: The Cavaliers plan to retain coach Kenny Atkinson even after the team was swept Monday night by the New York Knicks, two league sources told The Athletic. Atkinson guided the franchise to its first appearance in the conference finals since 2018 and first without LeBron James on the roster since 1992, but there was some question coming out of the series about potential changes Cleveland might make. Instead, no major changes to the front office or coaching staffs are now expected, per those source

item.title: Atkinson took the 2025 playoff failure hard and …
item.description: Atkinson took the 2025 playoff failure hard and rewatched every possession from the series against Indiana, looking for what went wrong. He was hard on his staff, numerous league sources said, in part because he felt he couldn’t be overly hard on the players. But at the same time, with Darius Garland and Max Strus out to start the regular season, Atkinson tried to lean on Evan Mobley to be more of an offensive threat. Both he and the team struggled with that priority.

item.title: Louis Williams criticizes Kenny Atkinson's …
item.description: Louis Williams criticizes Kenny Atkinson's analytics-heavy championship messaging: "And I've said it publicly, man, I love Kenny Atkinson to death. This is one of my favorite people that I've ever worked with in the business. He's responsible for getting me back on my feet in my career after I tore my ACL. We worked side by side every day of the week. But this messaging is just bad, man. I got to call a spade a spade. The messaging was bad. So, you allow a lot of analytical guys into the game an

item.title: Cavs will have interest in Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Windy on the Cavs roster: "I expect them to make some significant changes to it. I expect the Cavs to be mulling over...like I don't think it will be long before you start to hear Cavs attached to Giannis...would they consider entering that market? If they put Evan Mobley on the table...they drafted him to be another Giannis. What if they could turn him into Giannis with Harden and Donovan Mitchell on the roster"

item.title: Donovan Mitchell felt embarrassed getting swept
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell: "Stop the speculation. I want to come back. I want to work on getting that next deal" Spida also felt "embarrassed" on being swept

item.title: Kenny Atkinson will return as Cleveland Cavaliers head …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Kenny Atkinson will return as Cleveland Cavaliers head coach. Donovan Mitchell strongly endorsed Atkinson when asked about him. “I love Kenny. We love Kenny. We ride with Kenny. Ultimately, that’s all that matters. I know for a fact he’s from Long Island. He don’t give a damn.”

item.title: The insider said, “I don’t think it’ll be long before …
item.description: Windhorst added in a follow-up that Cleveland may explore acquiring Milwaukee Bucks superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo. The insider said, “I don’t think it’ll be long before you start to hear the Cavs attached to Giannis Antetokounmpo. If they put Evan Mobley on the table, what if they can turn him into Giannis?”

item.title: “I speak from experience,” Hammon said Tuesday. “Allen …
item.description: Those comments have generated some buzz after Brunson received the Larry Bird Trophy for MVP of the Eastern Conference finals after the Knicks swept the Cleveland Cavaliers on Monday night. He averaged 25.5 points and 7.8 assists in the series. The Knicks will face defending champion Oklahoma City or San Antonio in the finals. “I speak from experience,” Hammon said Tuesday. “Allen Iverson got MVP and he lost in the finals. I think the two best teams are probably in the West, but I’m up for being

item.title: Windhorst on Giannis Antetokounmpo to Boston: I have not heard that it's serious
item.description: According to ESPN's Brian Windhorst, there is no sign that serious talks are happening between the Celtics and Milwaukee Bucks regarding a possible blockbuster move for the former MVP. Speaking on ESPN Cleveland, Windhorst addressed the growing speculation linking Giannis to Boston but made it clear he has not heard anything concrete. "I know some people are linking him to Boston, but I have not heard that Giannis, Boston, and Milwaukee are serious about it. That's out there, but I haven't heard

item.title: Grizzlies opposed lottery reform over rule barring teams from three consecutive top-5 picks
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources said the Grizzlies voted against the new lottery reform solely because of the new rule disallowing a team from picking top 5 in 3 consecutive years. As @BobbyMarks42 says, the Grizzlies have the more favorable 2027 first-rounder of Cleveland, Minnesota and Utah, and because the Jazz again have a top-5 pick this year, their first in 2027 cannot be in the top-5 under new rules.

item.title: SGA currently holds the record for the worst drop off …
item.description: SGA currently holds the record for the worst drop off from regular season TS% efficiency to playoffs for MVPs SGA during the regular season had a historical 66.5 TS% which has dropped to 58.4% TS in the playoffs, an 8% decline. Previously the record was a 7.1% decline held by James Harden in 2017-2018 going from 61.9% TS to 54.8 TS%

item.title: Tristan Thompson retires from basketball: 'It feels like I'm a rookie again'
item.description: Tristan Thompson is keeping fans updated on his life outside of basketball. While sharing he had formally retired from the NBA after 14 seasons, the former Cleveland Cavaliers center reflected on life since stepping away from the court. "Honestly, it's been great," Tristan told ex Khloe Kardashian on the May 28 episode of her Khloe in Wonder Land podcast. "I always tell people it feels like I'm a rookie again."

item.title: Cavs president Koby Altman on Donovan Mitchell: He's uniquely ours and he's our guy
item.description: Cavs All-Star Donovan Mitchell will be eligible to sign a contract extension on July 7. Asked whether the Cavs will offer an extension to Mitchell this summer, Koby Altman said, "There hasn't been any question on if he wants to be here or if he will be here." When asked why he considers Mitchell an elite No. 1 player, Altman said, "Donovan is uniquely ours and he's our guy. When you are constantly an All-NBA guy ... you embrace that." "Do we think we can win a championship with him as our best p

item.title: Koby Altman expects Evan Mobley back with Cleveland: 'He's part of our future'
item.description: Speaking on whether Evan Mobley will be with the Cavs next season, Altman said, "Yeah, he's part of our future." With regard to speculation that the Cavs could package Mobley in a trade for Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo, Altman said he's "not going to speculate on any players outside of these walls. Since Evan's been here, we have the third-best record in the league."

item.title: Altman said that Cavs second-year player Jaylon Tyson …
item.description: Altman said that Cavs second-year player Jaylon Tyson "is going to be a big part of what we do, especially on (a rookie scale contract)." Altman said the team needs his tenacity, and that his ability to break into the rotation during his just second year in the pros speaks to his work ethic.

item.title: There were rumbles during the regular season that …
item.description: There were rumbles during the regular season that Cleveland would have interest in pursuing Quin Snyder — Donovan Mitchell's former coach in Utah — in the event of a coaching change there, but sources with knowledge of the Cavaliers' thinking have pushed back on such suggestions, insisting that Kenny Atkinson was always destined to retain his job for a third season.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell: We may or not win a ring here, but I've won in life
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell: "We may win a ring here. We may never win a ring here. But I've won in life"

item.title: Here...I love it here, I see growth, I see a runway, I …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell on his future with the Cavs: "I feel like this has been a consistent theme every single season I've been here...I love it here, I see growth, I see a runway, I believe, I've made Cleveland home. Outside of just basketball, I don't wanna go"

item.title: "I'm appreciative of him because he helped me get …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Donovan Mitchell on James Harden: "I'm appreciative of him because he helped me get somewhere that I've never been before. We live in a world where it's just so much easier to knock and hate. To see it firsthand, the work ethic, the passion, the IQ, the leadership, it's just like man just gets a bad rep"

item.title: Channing Frye on Donovan Mitchell: I don't think he is a true number one, you have to bring a one in there
item.description: Channing Frye: "You know the difference between Jalen Brunson and Donovan Mitchell is Brunson is a true number one. I do not think Donovan Mitchell is a true number one take you to the NBA Finals person. Do I think he is a great player? Yes. I think he is a two also. You have to bring a one in there, a real one."

item.title: Sixers hire Mike Gansey as President of Basketball Operations
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Cleveland Cavaliers general manager Mike Gansey has agreed to become the new President of Basketball Operations for the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Gansey -- who rose the ranks within the Cavaliers front office since joining in 2011-12 -- now lands the top executive position in Philadelphia.

item.title: He began working for the Cavs in 2012 and rose through …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Breaking: The Philadelphia 76ers are hiring Mike Gansey as president, sources told @hoopshype . Gansey was most recently general manager of the Cleveland Cavaliers. He began working for the Cavs in 2012 and rose through the ranks, including winning G League Executive of the Year.

item.title: He was very involved in targeting guys like Sam …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Always heard good things about Mike Gansey. He was very involved in targeting guys like Sam Merrill and Max Strus — pro personnel types of decisions. Should be a good hire for the Sixers.

item.title: I would expect him to opt out of that contract and …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "Harden's in a unique situation. He's got a $42 million player option and there's only $13 million of that guaranteed. So if Harden did opt in, Cleveland could certainly waive him and save a ton of money here. I would expect him to opt out of that contract and both sides to negotiate a a little bit of a longer contract, whether it be two years. And why opting out makes the most sense is that that would put Cleveland potentially under the second apron. If you drop 42 to let's say 30,

item.title: Donovan Mitchell on Cleveland: 'I love it here...I don't want to go'
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: I feel like this [contract extension talk] has been a consistent theme every single season I’ve been here. And I thought it would go away once I signed the first extension, but here we are. I said it [Monday night]: “I love it here.” I see growth. I see a runway. I believe. I’ve made Cleveland home outside of just basketball. I don’t want to go. I don’t really know how else to blatantly say it to people for them to believe me. item.title: Donovan Mitchell: 'We don't get to the conference finals without James Harden'
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: We don’t get to the conference finals without James Harden. We tried. I don’t know how else to put it, but we live in a world where it’s just so much easier to knock and hate. And pretty sure they’re going to hate me. They’re going to hate [Evan Mobley], they hate [head coach] Kenny [Atkinson]. They’re going to hate everybody, right? So, this right here isn’t me saying, “Don’t hate us.”

item.title: “It was a very painful ending. To go out like that, …
item.description: “When I woke up this morning, there is no easy way. … It was just like, ‘Damn, it’s over with,’ ” Mitchell told Andscape from the Cavaliers’ practice facility late Tuesday morning. “You just wake up like, ‘Damn, what am I doing? What do you do now?’ You just feel clueless, but not about just the loss. Being swept is always tough. It’s embarrassing, nasty. item.description: Point-blank, Cleveland Cavaliers president of basketball operations Koby Altman was prompted to answer whether or not Evan Mobley will be on the roster before next season begins. item.title: Thunder still a potential suitor for Evan Mobley?
item.description: There has long been chatter about the Thunder having interest in Cleveland big man Evan Mobley as well, but Cavaliers general manager Koby Altman insisted in an end-of-season news conference that Mobley wasn’t going anywhere.

item.title: “Donovan has been steadfast in how much he loves it …

With one year and a player option left on his current deal, Cavs president Koby Altman was asked about his status and a potential agreement, whether this summer or next offseason. “Donovan has been steadfast in how much he loves it here, and so there hasn't been any question of will he be here and does he want to be here,” Altman said. “So I think that's the big

item.title: Multiple Cavaliers executives would oppose Giannis Antetokounmpo trade involving Evan Mobley
item.description: The problem: The Cavs love Mobley, they believe in him and there are multiple members of the Cleveland brain trust that would be vehemently against a Giannis-Mobley swap, as the 2025 Defensive Player of the Year and Second-Team All-NBA selection is viewed as not only a future star but also a proverbial safety net when it comes to another rebuild. item.title: Remarkably, their offensive efficiency was the highest …
item.description: The Knicks just finished smoking the Eastern Conference competition, posting a 123.3 offensive rating on their way to the NBA Finals. Remarkably, their offensive efficiency was the highest intraconference mark posted by an East champion since the league's current playoff format was instituted in 2003, supplanting LeBron James' 2017 Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.description: ‪Justin Russo‬: New York Knicks are the second team in NBA postseason history to record 7 consecutive double-digit wins. Only the 2009 Cleveland Cavaliers (8) have posted a longer streak.

item.description: With the Cleveland Cavaliers currently hovering near the second apron, forward Dean Wade could be hard to retain this summer as an unrestricted free agent, with several teams having the non-taxpayer mid-level exception available expected to be interested in him. Last season, Wade hoped to sign an extension with the Cavaliers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: James Harden arrested in Houston for unlawfully carrying a weapon

The 36-year-old was taken into custody by the Houston Police Department at 3:41 a.m., according to court records obtained by The California Post, and booked on one misdemeanor charge of unlawful carrying weapons.

item.title: Harden, a former Los Angeles Clippers guard who now …
item.description: Harden, a former Los Angeles Clippers guard who now plays for the Cleveland Cavaliers, was accused of “unlawfully, intentionally and knowingly” having a handgun in his vehicle, according to a copy of the complaint. Authorities alleged in hte document that “the handgun was in plain view” and “was not carried in a holster.”

item.title: James Harden released from jail, scheduled to appear in court on June 22
item.description: Former Houston Rockets star James Harden was freed from jail Saturday morning after he was arrested on a misdemeanor gun violation hours earlier. According to Harris County records, Harden is accused of carrying an unconcealed weapon in a vehicle. Harden, 36, was driving a Mercedes that was among a group of five vehicles traveling through downtown at about 3:40 a.m. When one of the vehicles was stopped by police near 1600 Jefferson, Harden drove up behind in a Mercedes sedan, according to court

item.title: He was released on a general order bond a short time …
item.description: During an interaction with police, according to court records, the officer saw a gun in the cup holder of the car Harden was driving. Harden told police it was his, according to court records, and was arrested and booked at Harris County Jail. He was released on a general order bond a short time later with the promise that he'll appear in court on June 22.

item.title: Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites to win the 2027 NBA Finals at +250, followed by the Boston Celtics at +550. The New York Knicks, despite winning the championship on Saturday, have the fourth best odds at +700. The next block of teams have significantly longer odds beginning with the Indiana Pacers and Denver Nuggets at +2800, and the Minnesota Timbewolves, Los Angeles Lakers, Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers at +3000.

item.title: On Friday, June 19, the Cleveland Cavaliers, in …
item.description: On Friday, June 19, the Cleveland Cavaliers, in partnership with the City of Cleveland and Downtown Cleveland Inc., hosted the grand opening of the 2016 NBA Championship commemorative outdoor basketball court at Meet Me Here Plaza. Located across from Rocket Arena on Huron Road between East 4th Street and Ontario Street, the reimagined plaza features a new outdoor basketball court, walkways and seating designed to bring people together before, during and beyond Cavaliers game nights.

item.title: I believe Meleek Thomas would have been Cleveland's …
item.description: Jake Fischer: I believe Meleek Thomas would have been Cleveland's pick last night, if the Cavaliers didn't trade No. 29 to the Kings.

item.title: #Cavs were planning to sign Nick Martinelli to a …
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs were planning to sign Nick Martinelli to a two-way contract if he went undrafted. Martinelli just got drafted No. 55 instead.

item.title: The 6-foot-3 Lee agreed to a free-agent contract with …
item.description: From the time he first arrived on Princeton University’s campus as a lightly recruited point guard, Xaivian Lee believed he has what it takes to play in the NBA. Now he’s got a golden opportunity to find out. The 6-foot-3 Lee agreed to a free-agent contract with the Cleveland Cavaliers shortly after the NBA Draft. He’ll get a chance to prove himself in the NBA’s summer league and, most likely, in the G League during the 2026-27 season.

item.title: Rashaun Agee has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Texas A&M’s Rashaun Agee has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Cleveland Cavaliers, sources tell DraftExpress. Strongly built forward at 6’6 ¾", 236 lbs with a 7’1 ½" wingspan. Led the Aggies in scoring, rebounds, and blocks to kickstart the Bucky Ball era.

item.title: LeBron would have to want to sign with the Cavs. But …
item.description: Another wrench was thrown into this story June 24, when ESPN NBA insider Brian Windhorst made a bombshell claim during a segment on an ESPN Cleveland radio show. “The thought process in Los Angeles — and I think if the Cavs were interested in this, the Lakers would sit up in attention right now — that they would sign and trade LeBron for Jarrett Allen,” Windhorst said, per an X post from ESPN Cleveland. “And I think if the Cavs were willing to do that, they could have LeBron. Obviously, LeBron w

item.title: "I don't want to set expectations, but like we need to …
item.description: Jazz owner Ryan Smith: "I don't want to set expectations, but like we need to start winning. I think that's that's that's kind of where we're at. When we took over, I took over what was the number one team in the regular season and we got, I mean, it was interesting. My first day was like post-COVID. We were kind of into COVID and I had like two weeks to get a deal done with Rudy Gobert and Donovan Mitchell. It was really complicated because normally you have a long time to do this, but that tea

item.title: Dan Gilbert on reuniting with LeBron James: 'If you're both mutually on the same mission, it can work out'
item.description: Interviewer: How did two such strong personalities as Dan Gilbert—owner of the Cleveland Cavs, mega-billionaire, business owner, operator—and LeBron James have that kind of falling out and then make it work again? Dan Gilbert: Well, I flew to Miami, where he was with two or three of his advisers, and we had a couple-hour meeting. I think, at the end of the day, it was in both of our interests to do this because he wanted to come back. He wanted to come back home, and we’re the only place he coul

item.title: Dan Gilbert admits his infamous letter after LeBron James' departure was 'a stupid thing'
item.description: Did you prep for going into that meeting? Did you have a sense of what you were going to say, or did you just play it? Dan Gilbert: I’m sure I prepped it a little bit. Interviewer: Did you apologize early on, or before the meeting? Dan Gilbert: About the letter? Interviewer: Yeah. Dan Gilbert: Yeah. I mean, I admitted that the letter was a stupid thing. And for me personally, at that time, if you Googled my name—I mean, I think I’ve done some other stuff in my career—and that would be the only t

item.title: Dan Gilbert on fellow NBA owners: "I like the majority …
item.description: Dan Gilbert on fellow NBA owners: "I like the majority of them. There really are very few that I would say that I don't like. Most of them are pretty good guys, nice people."

item.title: He too, he used to pick on Mark Cuban, he used to pick …
item.description: Dan Gilbert: "David Stern was a phenomenal commissioner for 30 years. He built that league. But he too, he used to pick on Mark Cuban, he used to pick on me, he picked on the new guys."

item.title: Dan Gilbert on Adam Silver: "I give him an A-minus. I …
item.description: Dan Gilbert on Adam Silver: "I give him an A-minus. I think he's really good. You always got to have something to go for."

item.title: Dan Gilbert: Yeah. And it was stupid overall. If I had …
item.description: Interviewer: You wrote a very strongly worded letter. Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: I’m not going to make you read it. Dan Gilbert: No. Interviewer: But it talked about betrayal. Dan Gilbert: Well, what happened was, remember, he went on national television and did a show about where he was going to go play next. Interviewer: Yeah. Is that where you found out, though? Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: You found out when he announced it? Dan Gilbert: Yes. They never told us in advance. Interviewer:

item.title: So two people read it. Were you counseled not to put …
item.description: Interviewer: You gave him some heat. What were you thinking? Was that you doing it? Did some intern draft that letter? Dan Gilbert: I actually—you should have seen the first version. No, I’m kidding. Interviewer: Wait, did you have other people read this letter? Dan Gilbert: Yeah, I had one or two people, but they didn’t tell me what they should have told me. Interviewer: Who were the one or two people? Your brother? Dan Gilbert: No, one of the PR people. And I think maybe my ex-wife read it. In

item.title: Dan Gilbert reveals Comic Sans in his LeBron James letter was a joke by a team member weeks earlier
item.description: Well, the whole conversation around it was also around the font choice of the letter. Dan Gilbert: That was another thing. Interviewer: Comic Sans. Dan Gilbert: What was weird is that somebody on my team, just as a joke, I think the week or two before, changed my font to Comic Sans. Interviewer: What was your original font going to be? Dan Gilbert: Probably Times New Roman or something. So it wasn’t changed just for that letter. It was changed weeks ahead of that. Interviewer: So everything was

item.title: How’s your relationship now with LeBron? You guys …
item.description: How’s your relationship now with LeBron? You guys still— Dan Gilbert: I think it’s fine. We don’t see each other that much. He works in California. I work in Detroit and Cleveland. Interviewer: Yeah. Dan Gilbert: He came to a game or two last year. Interviewer: Was it hard for you to watch? In the game before the Eastern Conference Finals, it was— Dan Gilbert: Game seven. Interviewer: It was game seven, and it was the Cleveland Cavaliers— Dan Gilbert: And the Detroit Pistons. Interviewer: And yo

item.title: “Right now, the Cavs can only offer LeBron four …
item.description: Windhorst discussed James’ rumored reunion with the Cleveland Cavaliers on “The Pat McAfee Show,” and said he believes it’s highly unlikely to occur. “Right now, the Cavs can only offer LeBron four million bucks,” Windhorst said. “One of the things I’ve learned about LeBron is you never count his money and never spend his money. So, making assumptions that LeBron would take a lot less money, I see people making that assumption, and I just don’t think it’s smart.”

item.title: The only real way that I can see that the Cavs could …
item.description: Windhorst added, “He is still a max-level player. … The only real way that I can see that the Cavs could get LeBron the money would be to do a trade with Jarrett Allen. … But I don’t think that would improve the Cavs. … I don’t see the pathway.”

item.title: Timberwolves explored James Harden, Ja Morant as point guard options
item.description: At the same time, teams like Minnesota and Toronto — franchises that had been extremely aggressive in pursuing upgrades — found themselves with an opportunity to improve their fortunes. The Wolves, after sending Julius Randle to Brooklyn, were left with a $33 million trade exception and a cap book $66 million leaner, while continuing to signal intentions to add a quality point guard, sources say (a pursuit that at one time included Cleveland's James Harden and Memphis' Ja Morant). The Raptors, w

item.title: Richard Jefferson on thhe group chat dynamics of the …
item.description: Richard Jefferson on thhe group chat dynamics of the 2016 Cavs: "When we talk about the group chat from the team, there's like six of us, and it's been the same six. There was the full team group chat, then there was the guys that were J.R., Swish, Tristan, Kevin, Channing, and Bron. So that's the group of us. And then we start doing, hey guys, we should get together, do some golf and go do this, and then a conversation kind of got started is, and like, hey, it would be wrong if we didn't invite

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers are nearing a deal to sell a …
item.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers are nearing a deal to sell a 5% to 10% stake in the NBA franchise to private equity firm Blue Owl. The deal values the NBA team and Cleveland WNBA team set to start play in 2028 at a combined $5.5 billion, according to someone familiar with the agreement. Blue Owl’s sports strategy fund buys limited partner stakes in NBA franchises, a fund originally named Dyal HomeCourt. The fund already has stakes in the Atlanta Hawks, Charlotte Hornets, Minnesota Timberwolves and Sacra

item.title: Dan Gilbert hired Allen & Company to explore selling …
item.description: Last year, Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert hired Allen & Company to explore selling up to 15% of his NBA franchise. Gilbert will retain his majority stake in the team he bought in 2005 for $375 million, which was a record at the time for a standalone NBA franchise. In December, Sportico valued the Cavs at $4.86 billion in its NBA team valuations, which ranks 16th in the league.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson: When you're one of the final four teams in the NBA, I think you're doing something right
item.description: What are you most proud of this season? Kenny Atkinson: I think we were 17-16 at one point. It looked like the wheels were falling off. Just how we kept it together, with expectations being so high, and we started playing a lot of young guys. We got through that with great leadership, starting with Donovan Mitchell, then Evan Mobley, even before De'Andre Hunter came. Those guys kind of kept the ship afloat. As we got healthier, we really went on some good runs. Listen, success is relative, right

item.title: The thing that changed that equation was James …
item.description: Was the equation harder to figure out, or did it become easier once James Harden came into the fold? Kenny Atkinson: It's a great point, and I was worried about that. How do we get this thing going in two and a half months? The thing that changed that equation was James Harden's IQ and ability. And I always know James is a system, just being by himself. But it was more like he's played in so many different scenarios in the NBA and his career that he adapted. It's probably the fastest adaptation

item.title: Luke Travers will play for the Milwaukee Bucks in the …
item.description: Olgun Uluc: Luke Travers will play for the Milwaukee Bucks in the 2026 NBA Summer League, sources told ESPN. The 6'8 Australian forward was the 56th overall pick in the 2022 NBA Draft; he spent the last two seasons on a two-way contract with the Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein getting a mutual option likely …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Isaiah Hartenstein getting a mutual option likely means the third year is a player option with a partial guarantee, like James Harden’s current contract. The ~$3M in payroll savings reduces the Thunder’s tax penalty by $21M and leaves them about $15M above the second apron.

item.title: I want to see if the Cavs are extending Max Strus or …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "I would keep an eye on Max Strus. Dean Wade is going to be expensive, I think more expensive than the Cavs were hoping. I think Keon Ellis could be gone unless they lose Dean Wade. If they lose Dean Wade, I think they re-engineer the concept of Keon Ellis. Max Strus's extension eligible has one year at $19 million left on his contract. I want to see if the Cavs are extending Max Strus or if they have to trade Max Strus. And the reason you would trade Max Strus is so that you co

item.title: I heard from a whole lot of coaches, a few were ready …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "Micah Nori accepted a contract as a renter coach. It's a one plus one plus one contract. It's a three-year deal with team options for the second and third year. I heard from a whole lot of coaches, a few were ready to go on the record and backed out. Props to JB Bickerstaff, who is the president of the NBCA, the National Basketball Coaches Association. He felt like it was his duty as the president of that organization to take a firm stance on this. He felt like this contract was a

item.title: "The most annoying part was Delly and Bron waking up …
item.description: Richard Jefferson: "The most annoying part was Delly and Bron waking up to work out every day. That was the most annoying part. It's like, really, you're going to go work out?"

item.title: "When we saw Delly, we thought he grew two inches. …
item.description: Channing Frye: "When we saw Delly, we thought he grew two inches. This dude is built like a WWE wrestler. He is the most yoked I have ever seen him. He said a few years ago he completely changed his whole routine and how he is approaching the game. Delly did work out. He was up with us at night and he was up by himself in the morning. This dude is dedicated to his craft. We love seeing him. You got a glimpse of his personality when he lets loose, but he is always thinking about basketball."

item.title: "Trust me, those guys asked LeBron what he was …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "Trust me, those guys asked LeBron what he was planning on doing and LeBron did not tell them," - @WindhorstESPN on LeBron James' future plans.

item.title: “It might even be more likely than not that he leaves …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “It might even be more likely than not that he leaves Los Angeles than stays with the Lakers,” - @JakeLFischer on the latest rumblings around LeBron James 👀👀👀

item.title: “They got soul issues. They got I don’t like you …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “They got soul issues. They got I don’t like you issues and I don’t want to be here with you issues,” - @channingfrye on why he doesn’t think LeBron should go back to the Cavs 😳😳😳

item.title: Thomas, who was selected 34th overall in the second …
item.description: With that in mind, after finding Jaylon Tyson in the first round of the 2024 NBA Draft to give themselves a young, cheaper rotation option, the Cavaliers appear to feel equally bullish about rookie Meleek Thomas, who turns 20 on August 6th. Thomas, who was selected 34th overall in the second round of the draft, is expected to agree to a standard NBA contract with the second-round exception, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Cavs, 76eres, Lakers, Spurs, Suns, Pistons, Blazers among teams interested in Dean Wade?
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers forward Dean Wade is expected to have various suitors in free agency, including the Cavaliers, Philadelphia 76ers, Los Angeles Lakers, San Antonio Spurs, Phoenix Suns, Detroit Pistons, and Portland Trail Blazers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Max Strus and Dennis Schroeder have been mentioned in …
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Cavaliers navigate James Harden’s return and Wade’s free agency, Cleveland’s Max Strus and Dennis Schroeder have been mentioned in trade talks with the franchise currently projected over the second apron.

item.title: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, …
item.description: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, including interest from the Hawks, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Philadelphia 76ers, and Cleveland Cavaliers, league sources told HoopsHype. Expect the Australian center to command offers north of the bi-annual exception in free agency.

item.title: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back …
item.description: Law Murray: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back in 2018. Like, really, really bad. And when LeBron left Cleveland for the Lakers, Joel had one of my favorite of his troll tweets when he was like, I'm paraphrasing, but he was like, they're not going to like you in Los Angeles. That's Kobe's town. And that dude LeBron, it's been that's how it's felt. Like he's been polarizing in a Los Angeles uniform because you got Bron fans, you got Lakers fans, you got Kobe fans. It's a whole

item.title: Patrick Beverley: James Harden is better than Dwyane Wade
item.description: Patrick Beverley: I can never disrespect Dwyane Wade, but if you ask me an honest question of who's better, I'm not just going to be on some like ignorant sh*t. Like I'mma go like, "Oh, James Harden is better." Like he's a better scorer. He's a better passer. He shoots the three better. He gets to the free throw line more. He has more triple-doubles. He has scored the more opponents. He has more MVPs. He's never missed the playoffs, more playoffs experience. And the more… I mean, Wade has a ring

item.title: “I think the Cavs might have been involved a little …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “I think the Cavs might have been involved a little bit, I heard in the Miles Bridges discussions,” - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs. Windy notes that the Cavs could trade Max Strus for an upgrade.

item.title: James Harden opts out of deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Cleveland Cavaliers' James Harden is declining his $42.3 million player option for 2026-27 and the sides are working through a new multiyear deal together, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Thomas Bryant returns to Cavs on a one-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Thomas Bryant intends to sign a one-year deal to return to the Cleveland Cavaliers, sources tell ESPN. Bryant averaged 12.2 minutes and 6.2 points over 60 games for the Cavs, who worked with Mark Bartelstein and Zach Kurtin of @PrioritySports on the new contract.

item.title: Basically what will happen is you will probably see …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "James Harden declines his $42 million player option. It was a mutual option. There was $13 million guaranteed. It was a no-brainer. Basically what will happen is you will probably see Harden's number between $32-33 million. There will be a second year attached to basically make up the money that he lost. Expect James Harden back."

item.title: #Cavs and Dean Wade are having ongoing negotiations …
item.description: Chris Fedor: On the eve of NBA free agency, sources tell @clevelanddotcom that #Cavs and Dean Wade are having ongoing negotiations and dialogue. Both sides plan to keep working to see if a deal is possible before free agency Tuesday. Neither side has closed the door on a return to Cleveland.

item.title: Cavaliers have interest in second reunion with LeBron James
item.description: Chris Haynes: Cleveland Cavaliers have interest in a second reunion with LeBron James with the appeal of the franchise’s greatest player finishing his career where it all started, league sources tell me.

item.title: "I definitely think it's something to keep an eye on, …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "I definitely think it's something to keep an eye on, that's one to keep an eye on," - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs potentially trading Max Strus.

item.title: "They are working on trades, I'm not going to tell you …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "They are working on trades, I'm not going to tell you who they're working on because I can't deal with it in my life," - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs right now 🤣🤣🤣

item.title: Cavaliers, Heat, Warriors expected to emerge as suitors for LeBron James
item.description: SportsCenter: I'm told this is a happiness-led decision for LeBron James. ... Wherever he lands in free agency, it will not be driven by money." "It's unclear what the timeline will be. Right now, he's in Ohio spending time with his family. He's gonna make a decision on his own time. But there's no question all of the NBA's contending teams are in the picture for LeBron James. Rich Paul will call LeBron with expressed interest as soon as free agency opens up tonight. Around the league, teams exp

item.title: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the …
item.description: Heat Central: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the Cavs, and you look at the Heat. He’s been in those places. He knows the terrain. I think it’s too soon to say anyone is the front runner” — @TheSteinLine on LeBron destinations

item.title: Cavaliers open to the idea of a LeBron James return
item.description: Sources tell cleveland.com that the Cavs are open to the idea of a LeBron James return. Of course, signing the franchise legend for one final stint isn’t exactly straightforward given their salary cap situation. Although cap-strapped Cleveland is currently $29.1 million below the first apron and $42.1 million below the second apron, those numbers don’t reflect the expected multiyear deal James Harden is expected to sign in the near future.

item.title: Max Strus, Dennis Schroeder on the move?
item.description: Since Cleveland’s season ended in a Game 4 loss to the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference finals, sources say the team has had discussions about the possibility of trading Max Strus and Dennis Schroder. Strus is set to make $16.6 million in the final year of his contract. Schroder is still owed nearly $31 million over the next two seasons. Those moves would help Cleveland’s salary cap situation. If the Cavs get below the punitive aprons, they could also theoretically orchestrate a sign-an

item.title: @clevelanddotcom that Cleveland has remained engaged …
item.description: Chris Fedor: Despite the LeBron James news and #Cavs being interested, sources tell @clevelanddotcom that Cleveland has remained engaged in conversations with free agent Dean Wade about the possibility of him returning.

item.title: James Harden holding off on signing new deal with Cavs to see if they can get LeBron James
item.description: On Monday, Harden declined his $42.3 million player option and is now a free agent. He is expected to return to Cleveland on a new contract, but Harden is waiting to sign to give the Cavs the flexibility they need to make a move like adding LeBron for next season, according to a league source who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations.

item.title: Cavs looking to move Max Strus
item.description: To that end, the Cavs are trying to trade forward Max Strus, league sources told The Athletic, so they could sign LeBron — or perhaps another player — into that midlevel exception. Strus is slated to make $16.6 million next season. If Harden signs for $30 million, and if Cleveland can deal Strus and take back no salary in return, it would be able to give James the midlevel exception.

item.title: The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James main priority is going to a team that has a chance at winning, per @ChrisBHaynes "I'm told he's going to be open minded and patient for his next team.... The one component a team is going to have to have to secure his service from what i'm told, is the team has to be competitive and have a realistic chance at winning.... The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can pretty much look and see of those 3 who has the best shot at winning... Money wasn't mentioned"

item.title: Timberwolves have interest in LeBron James?
item.description: Cleveland and Miami are often cited as realistic landing spots as well, and the Minnesota Timberwolves are known to have interest if he felt like shocking the basketball world with that sort of move.

item.title: Being told that barring a last minute change here, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Being told that barring a last minute change here, Dean Wade will test the market in unrestricted free agency at 6 p.m ET, where Wade is expected to field interest from several playoff teams.

item.title: LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker …
item.description: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar: @KingJames LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker fans should appreciate his performance for the last 8 years. If LeBron goes home to Cleveland, it won’t matter if they win the World Championship or not because his hometown fans will get their opportunity to say their goodbyes to one of the greatest ever. And who knows one day he may become Mayor of Cleveland !!

item.title: Lebron farewell tour in Ohio going to be popcorn
item.description: Dwight Howard: Lebron farewell tour in Ohio going to be popcorn 🍿

item.title: “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have interest in LeBron James, per @TheSteinLine “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but there is a line…. Cleveland is certainly going to try and convince him of a 3rd marriage, Miami has interest…. This might not necessarily happen tonight”

item.title: NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 teams each got a $4.85 million payout. Cavaliers owe NBA-leading $68.671 million in tax payments. Warriors will pay $67.91 million as a repeater tax payer. Knicks owe $44.44 million after finishing $19.5 mil over tax line.

item.title: “Did you know LeBron James loves Charlotte?” McGee …
item.description: ESPN personality Ryan McGee -- who just so happens to have deep Carolina roots -- talked about James’ love for Bojangles during a 2024 appearance on the Pat McAfee Show. “Did you know LeBron James loves Charlotte?” McGee asked McAfee during that appearance. “And the reason he loves Charlotte is because Bojangles -- I’m not making this up -- Bojangles chicken. “When LeBron James was a rookie, the deal was he would leave games at the old Hive ... the takeaway food you get on the plane after a game

item.title: Jaylen Brown is out there,”
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “James Harden opted out yesterday. He from what I understand is willing to partner with the Cavs to try and figure out how to improve their roster. Jaylen Brown is out there,” - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs

item.title: Robert Williams' extension is only fully guaranteed in first year
item.description: Jake Fischer: Dean Wade's deal with the Sixers has a partial guarantee in Year 4, per source. In Portland, I'm told Robert Williams' extension is fully guaranteed at $14 million for this season, but just $5 million guaranteed in Year 2, which becomes fully guaranteed if he plays 50 games this 2026-27 campaign. Then, Year 3 is entirely non-guaranteed, but becomes fully guaranteed if Williams plays 50 games in 27-28. Wonky...

item.title: Other teams who showed significant interest in Wade …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Dean Wade's 4-year, $39 million deal with the 76ers includes a fourth-year partial guarantee. Other teams who showed significant interest in Wade included the Cleveland Cavaliers, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, and Detroit Pistons, sources said.

item.title: Shams: LeBron James in no rush to make decision
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on LeBron James future: “One quote really sticks out to me. Rich Paul on June 2025 to me, ‘LeBron James wants to compete for a championship, he knows the Lakers are building for the future.’ When I talk to teams around the league it’s a few teams that really get narrowed. It’s the Golden State Warriors, Cleveland Cavaliers, and the Miami Heat as a perspective destination. I’m told LeBron it’s not in a rush.”

item.title: There’s already a history with both Miami and …
item.description: Heat Central: Shams explains why Miami would be enticing to LeBron: “They have Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo, and Erik Spoelstra. They’re the Miami Heat. There’s already a history with both Miami and Cleveland if LeBron wants to go to either destination and go back to the Eastern conference. But there’s a reason GS formulated a plan over the last week that included Draymond Green surprisingly opting out”

item.title: This is more than Ariel Hukporti's minimum salary, so …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: This is more than Ariel Hukporti's minimum salary, so this will likely come from the Sixers' mid-level exception. They have $6 million remaining after signing Dean Wade last night.

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers have signed 2026 NBA Draft …
item.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers have signed 2026 NBA Draft pick Meleek Thomas, Cavaliers President of Basketball Operations Koby Altman announced today from Cleveland Clinic Courts. In a related move, the team has also signed Ernest Udeh Jr. to a two-way contract.

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers signed Meleek Thomas, the 34th …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Cleveland Cavaliers signed Meleek Thomas, the 34th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, to a four-year, $9.3 million deal, league sources told @hoopshype . Thomas gets the first three years fully guaranteed for a total of $6.4 million. Agent Sam Permut of Roc Nation finalized the deal.

item.title: So now that he has done that, I will open the window …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "I have a confession. I have been thinking for nine months about LeBron playing for the Denver Nuggets. I refused to talk about it during the season, but I just thought as soon as they didn't extend LeBron last year and they extended Luka, I kind of knew it was probably going to come to an end. I thought about the Denver Nuggets because him alongside Jokic is an incredibly perfect fit. They are so intelligent, the way they see the game. And here is the thing, it was all predicat

item.title: Spurs not expected to go after LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Spurs’ deal with Tobias Harris slams the point home, but league sources say San Antonio is not expected to pursue LeBron James. @BennettDurando reported that Denver, by contrast, IS joining Golden State, Cleveland and Miami in this chase. More NBA: tinyurl.com/pebetpkn

item.title: "It's about a 50/50 right now. It's between Cleveland …
item.description: 95.7 The Game: "It's about a 50/50 right now. It's between Cleveland and Golden State." Warriors insider @NickFriedell on what the chances are that Lebron James joins the Warriors (@WillardAndDibs)

item.title: LeBron James has 'no timetable' for free agency decision
item.description: Shams Charania: After LeBron James' decision to leave the Lakers on Tuesday, he returned back to the golf course. And so clearly LeBron James is not in a rush right now to make a decision. He's evaluating everything, but his biggest priority in all this is finding a team where he feels like he's going to have a chance to play meaningful basketball, compete for a championship. My understanding is this will not be a financially driven money decision. And so his agent Rich Paul has been getting abo

item.title: “For a long time, that was what people recognized me …
item.description: In 2017, while working as a sideline reporter in Cleveland, she started the podcast Road Trippin’ alongside then-active Cavaliers players Richard Jefferson and Channing Frye. Ex-NBA center and ESPN analyst Kendrick Perkins later became an official cohost in 2024. Road Trippin’ is considered one of the first and longest-running player-led podcasts. “It changed the trajectory of my career,” Clifton told Front Office Sports about the podcast. “For a long time, that was what people recognized me for

item.title: Critique basketball all you want… but disease is …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: Critique basketball all you want… but disease is insane… we gotta stop letting people just say whatever… cmon man! NBA Central: NBA sources believe Jaylen Brown thinks he’s the smartest person in the room, per @colincowherd: “I had two NBA sources … two people in the league, one an executive, one a scout, say that Jaylen Brown has — it’s a disease. He suddenly thinks he’s the smartest guy in every room he’s in … You make a lot of money, suddenly you’re absolutely sure, you don’

item.title: #Cavs have announced their 2026 NBA Summer League …
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs have announced their 2026 NBA Summer League roster. Notably absent is Tyrese Proctor, who is set to play in FIBA Qualifiers with the Australian National Team beginning tomorrow.

item.title: Jonathan Kuminga drawing interest from Hawks, Cavaliers, Bucks, Kings, Lakers
item.description: Despite helping the Atlanta Hawks win two playoff games against the New York Knicks, the franchise declined Jonathan Kuminga’s $24.3 million team option. Sources say this decision was strategically done to create financial flexibility, including being able to utilize the full non-taxpayer mid-level exception, while still maintaining Kuminga’s Bird Rights, so he can potentially sign a contract at a lower number than his team option amount. It also opens up sign-and-trade possibilities for Atlanta

item.title: The Lakers obviously face financial constraints but …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Lakers obviously face financial constraints but league sources say they continue to explore the feasibility of adding Jonathan Kuminga to fill a wing need. Cleveland and Milwaukee have also expressed Kuminga interest and a return to Atlanta has not been ruled out.

item.title: “Rich Paul got a dozen of calls, pitches, offers …
item.description: NBA Base: Shams: “Rich Paul got a dozen of calls, pitches, offers already from contending teams, teams with aspirations… right now, the three that I mentioned — Golden State, Cleveland, Miami — those are seen as some of the types of teams that make logical sense for LeBron James.”

item.title: It must be said that there is most certainly a …
item.description: It had been anticipated for literally weeks that ever-aggressive Golden State would be at the front of the line to try to sign James if he actually created that opportunity by parting ways with the Lakers. Yet it must be said that there is most certainly a tangible line of teams forming. Ditto for a growing sense that LeBron's hometown Cleveland Cavaliers — despite financial limitations to rival Golden State's — might just have the capability to ultimately barge their way past everyone even thou

item.title: He go back to Cleveland and he got a chance to really …
item.description: Carmelo Anthony on LeBron James: "If I'm going back to Cleveland, this means I'm giving my all. We already see what he does every season, and we keep being amazed by the shit. He go back to Cleveland and he got a chance to really win the fucking championship, he's going to give everything, 23 years of the 40 years of basketball into one season, and he instantly elevates the Cleveland Cavaliers."

item.title: The Hawks remain open to re-signing Kuminga, sources …
item.description: The Lakers know, though, that they badly still need more help on the perimeter and were thus trying Wednesday — despite clear financial limitations — to see if a pathway exists to acquiring unrestricted free agent Jonathan Kuminga. The Hawks remain open to re-signing Kuminga, sources say, and there's been interest from Milwaukee and Cleveland along with the Lakers.

item.title: Paul added that an "X factor" for Philadelphia is its …
item.description: Paul added that an "X factor" for Philadelphia is its front office featuring executive vice president of basketball operations Jameer Nelson -- who was a contemporary of James, playing together on the All-Star team and against one another in the playoffs -- and president of basketball operations Mike Gansey -- who played against James in high school and was a member of the Cleveland Cavaliers' front office during James' second stint with the franchise. Paul also said he and James "love" the Sixe

item.title: Paul added that the Cavs would have been even more …
item.description: "[Donovan] Mitchell, [James] Harden, [Evan] Mobley, [Jarrett] Allen. Dan and Grant -- which is the Gilberts. Plus Koby [Altman], he was there when LeBron won there," Paul said. "Big, big, big X factor, let me talk to you about Brandon Weems," who is the Cavs' assistant GM under the president of basketball operations, Altman, and was a childhood friend and James' high school teammate. "Brandon Weems is basically LeBron's brother," Paul said. "Now, he worked his own way [up], nobody gave him anyth

item.title: Shams: LeBron James deciding between Cavs, Heat, Nuggets, Sixers, Wolves, Warriors
item.description: Fullcourtpass: LeBron James will be making his free agent decision between the following six NBA teams, per @ShamsCharania - Cavaliers - Heat - Nuggets - Sixers - Timberwolves - Warriors

item.title: He’s expected to view the Miami Heat as a more viable …
item.description: James could experience a more organic farewell tour in his third stop in Cleveland, but he has fielded skepticism that he would join the Cavaliers on a veteran’s minimum deal. He’s expected to view the Miami Heat as a more viable option after they acquired Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks. The Heat could also offer a taxpayer mid-level exception between $11 or $12 million per years.

item.title: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources …
item.description: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources say, all still believe that they are in this race to some degree and getting legitimate consideration from James and his camp alongside the Cavaliers, Warriors and Heat.

item.title: Teams believe Cavs are favorites for LeBron James?
item.description: Yet there is certainly a growing belief among teams competing for James' signature that the lure of the 41-year-old playing out his final chapter with the team that drafted him — compared to, say, early favorites Golden State — is looking more and more like the scenario to beat.

item.title: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Cleveland …
item.description: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Cleveland Cavaliers in 2026-27 have surged from 39.9% to 51.7% in the past 6 hours on Polymarket, an 11.8-point move.

item.title: LeBron James with his St. Vincent St Mary teammates - …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: LeBron James with his St. Vincent St Mary teammates - including Cavs assistant GM Brandon Weems - in Akron last night 👀

item.title: "It's being reported, not by me, but it makes sense …
item.description: 92.3 The Fan: "It's being reported, not by me, but it makes sense and I had past conversations that would align with this, that if LeBron came here, there would be interest in Bronny. I'm more looking at what the Cavs do or how they move on from (Dennis Schroder and Max Strus) more so than I am what big move can they make beside LeBron." 🚨 @joevardon w/ @RuiterWrongFAN and @LanceReisland on the #Cavs positioning for LeBron James

item.title: I just think that there are viable cases to be made …
item.description: Joe Vardon: "I told Rich that I thought this was the best or the most unique and that that had never been done before, what he did. It was absolutely incredible and unique, that entire message and the entire podcast, just to be so open about it. I think a lot of teams really are in the mix. I just think that there are viable cases to be made for Cleveland, Philadelphia, Miami, Golden State, and Dallas and the Timberwolves. I think those all those teams really are in play and I just feel like Ric

item.title: So I am like, shout out to Kevin Love for doing it. He …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "I got to give a big shout out to Kevin Love. Kevin Love put it together. If you had to look at your bingo card and guess who would put it together back then, you would have said Kevin Love. Kevin Love has been in Miami. He has been taking that leadership role and he has done the group chat. So I am like, shout out to Kevin Love for doing it. He put his mind to it, got his team together, and we all showed up. It was amazing."

item.title: Tristan Thompson: "If they win that (in 2016), …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "If they win that (in 2016), Draymond Green get MVP. He had 30, 10, and 10 or something like that."

item.title: So there is a chance ☝️
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis almost had a heart attack when Neon stream trolled him saying LeBron signed to Cleveland 👀 Giannis when he found out it’s fake: So there is a chance ☝️

item.title: Rival teams think LeBron James is headed to Cleveland
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “I’ve talked to people in Philly, and I’m like what do you think? ‘We’re afraid it’s Cleveland,’ - everybody I talked to is like ‘we’re afraid it’s Cleveland,’ - nobody seems like they have optimism,” - @WindhorstESPN on the latest with LeBron James 👀👀

item.title: The two teams that we seem to hear the most chatter …
item.description: Matt George: The two teams that we seem to hear the most chatter about in terms of potential landing destinations for DeMar DeRozan, the Miami Heat, who of course just traded for Giannis, and the Cleveland Cavaliers, who are a team I long thought could use the services of DeMar, especially in that second unit off of the bench. Of course, we've always heard LA ties, right? He is from LA, from Compton, very much a Los Angeles guy. So, we've always talked about the possibility of DeMar going home.

item.title: Joe Vardon: Count the Cavs in that group. Cleveland …
item.description: Joe Vardon: Count the Cavs in that group. Cleveland has one or two roster moves to make to be able to pay LeBron James $15 million next season instead — unloading Strus and Schröder — but hasn’t done either yet. I am told they, like other teams pursuing James, are in a holding pattern. The Cavs might make those moves even if James doesn’t pick them. They also might not make those moves if he picks them. Ostensibly, trading Strus for nothing (to increase James’ salary) makes them worse after the

item.title: Donovan Mitchell would embrace Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James
item.description: Donovan Mitchell also would embrace the Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James if James makes the decision to sign with Cleveland, according to sources. The Cavaliers are among several leading suitors for James, joining Miami, Golden State, Philadelphia and Minnesota.

item.title: "LeBron can influence the balance of power in the …
item.description: The Eastern Conference hierarchy could easily shift again, should LeBron James choose a third tour with Cleveland -- which would wipe away some of the stink from the Cavaliers' largely noncompetitive conference finals -- or pick another East team such as Philadelphia. "LeBron can influence the balance of power in the conference," the executive said. "He's not a driver, not on a night-to-night basis. [But] having him around, he picks and chooses his spots. "I wonder how he would fit with Kenny At

item.title: Year 1 - $60.9 million Year 2 - $65.8 million Year 3 - …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: Donovan Mitchell's contract extension per year: Year 1 - $60.9 million Year 2 - $65.8 million Year 3 - $70.6 million Year 4 - $75.5 million Spida got PAID.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell could have waited until next summer …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell could have waited until next summer for an additional season worth an extra $80 million on the deal (five years and $353 million), according to ESPN's Bobby Marks. He instead committed on a long-term extension on the first day he was eligible for a new contract to stamp his pledge to Cleveland. Mitchell was entering the last guaranteed season of his contract ahead of a 2027 player option, which now gets replaced by the new deal.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell to sign four-year, $273 million max extension with Cavaliers
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: Cleveland Cavaliers All-NBA star Donovan Mitchell has agreed on a four-year, $273 million maximum contract extension that includes a player option for the 2030-31 season and a full trade kicker, CAA's Co-Head of Basketball, Austin Brown, tells ESPN.

item.title: Mitchell could have waited until next summer for an …
item.description: Shams Charania: After leading the Cavaliers to the Eastern Conference finals, Mitchell could have waited until next summer for an additional fifth season worth an extra $80 million but instead committed early on a long-term extension on the first day he was eligible for a new contract to stamp his pledge to the city of Cleveland. This marks the second extension for Mitchell since arriving to Cleveland in 2022.

item.title: Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert and president of …
item.description: Shams Charania: Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert and president of basketball operations Koby Altman spoke with Mitchell and Brown once the seven-time NBA All-Star was able to land a new extension, and reached terms quickly. All parties have been in lockstep on the future direction. Mitchell has played a significant part in the Cavaliers’ conversations to improve the roster, and supportive of the front office, and that has continued this summer.

item.title: Player is eligible to still receive 35% of the salary …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Mentioned this with @ZachLowe_NBA Change to the 35% max rule * Player is eligible to still receive 35% of the salary cap if 10 years of service (Donovan Mitchell) or has reached the All-NBA criteria (Tatum SGA, Brown) * However, if the player was drafted or traded within the first 4 years in the NBA (SGA for example), only 30% of the salary applies to the cap. * In the case of Jaylen Brown, $48.9M this season and not $57M applies The rule is a bigger version of what applies already

item.title: "Yes, absolutely, he knows what he's going to do,"
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "Yes, absolutely, he knows what he's going to do," - @WindhorstESPN on LeBron James' decision.

item.title: Windhorst: The 'vibes' point towards LeBron James signing with Cleveland
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "If you ask me, 'Are the vibes pointing towards Cleveland?,' Yeah, the vibes are pointing towards Cleveland," - @WindhorstESPN with the latest on LeBron Watch 2026.

item.title: “The Heat, Warriors, Cavaliers, 76ers, Timberwolves, …
item.description: Heat Central: Marc Stein on the interested LeBron teams and their free agent spending: “The Heat, Warriors, Cavaliers, 76ers, Timberwolves, and Nuggets, all of these teams are kind of watching every dollar right now to try and maintain absolute maximum flexibility in the event that LeBron says ‘yes, I’d like to come there.’”

item.title: "I think James Harden is embracing the concept,"
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "I think James Harden is embracing the concept," - @WindhorstESPN on the potential Harden and LeBron James fit. Windy mentions that Harden could sign a 3-year deal with the Cavs.

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers also continue to have interest …
item.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers also continue to have interest in Kuminga, league sources told ESPN. Kuminga has a high level of comfort with Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson, who coached Kuminga during his first three NBA seasons as a lead assistant for the Golden State Warriors. The Cavaliers, however, are currently wrapped in the LeBron James sweepstakes, leaving the Lakers as the most aggressive pursuer prior to the James domino falling.

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers have re-signed center Thomas …
item.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers have re-signed center Thomas Bryant, Cavaliers President of Basketball Operations Koby Altman announced today.

item.title: Shams: Sixers, Heat, Cavs appear to be top 3 teams for LeBron James
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat, Cleveland Cavaliers, and 76ers appear to be the top 3 teams in the LeBron sweepstakes, per @ShamsCharania “When the Sixers got Jaylen Brown I did some research and he (LeBron) is taking their pitch very seriously. When I talk to teams now I have a hierarchy of Cleveland, Miami, Philadelphia, and then some teams on the peripheral”

item.title: DeMar DeRozan interested in going back to Raptors?
item.description: While DeRozan is interested in reuniting with the Raptors, NBA insider Jake Fischer revealed that the former NBA star might likely join teams that will fail to land James in this year's free agency. “He’s almost certainly going to be on a vet minimum somewhere else. Could it be Toronto? ... DeMar has interest in going back to the Raptors, but I think DeMar’s most likely landing spots are going to be teams that don’t get LeBron James," Fischer revealed on Bleacher Report. “I think the Miamis, Cle

item.title: I am told Donovan Mitchell has been hands-on with his …
item.description: "We know the Cavs are among the leaders to land LeBron James. How does Mitchell's extension impact that decision?" Shams Charania: "It certainly helps because here you have a franchise cornerstone in Cleveland now doubling down on the franchise. This is the second extension Donovan Mitchell has signed since joining the Cavs in 2022. I am told Donovan Mitchell has been hands-on with his approach to trying to help the Cavs and work with the Cavs on roster upgrades after their run to the Eastern Co

item.title: “People in the league think that it’s pointing towards …
item.description: But Windhorst has switched his sentiment up, as he suggested that the Cleveland Cavaliers appear to be the favorite to sign James right now during a July 7 appearance on ESPN Cleveland. Although this report comes with an important caveat. “People in the league think that it’s pointing towards Cleveland. But they are operating off vibes,” Windhorst said. “And if you ask me, ‘Are the vibes pointing towards Cleveland?’ Yeah, the vibes are pointing towards Cleveland. But it’s just vibes.”

item.title: "I would lean the Cavaliers if I had to bet right this …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "I would lean the Cavaliers if I had to bet right this second. But the Wolves, I checked again in the last 12-ish hours. It's not like the Wolves have been told they are out. I do think it's noteworthy that on the latest episode of Rich Paul's podcast, which is also available on YouTube, he singled out the Wolves, but also when discussing the Wolves, name-dropped Tim Connelly, then brought up the ownership group, Marc Lore, Alex Rodriguez. Connect those dots. It's not some sort o

item.title: James Harden, Cavaliers on same page as Cleveland pursues LeBron James and roster upgrades
item.description: "Let's get to one guy that played with the Cavs this past season, that is James Harden. He opted out of his deal with Cleveland. What can you tell us about his future with the Cavs?" Shams Charania: "James Harden will be going back to the Cavaliers just depending on how this shakes out with LeBron James and other upgrades the Cavs are able to make. He will be back, my understanding is on a multi-year deal. Whether that is a two-year deal, a three-year deal, a lot of that shakes out after the Cav

item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "He's having a great time in Akron this summer, I don't remember him being in Akron this long in the summer since after his first year in Miami," - @WindhorstESPN breaking down why the vibes point towards Cleveland.

item.title: I think CLE and MIA are in their own category. To this …
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst believes Miami and Cleveland are in their own category for LeBron’s next team: “One other thing I think is going to come down here that LeBron will have to decide is how much he cares about what people will think if he goes to a team he’s never played for before. Basically if he chooses somebody besides CLE or MIA. CLE or MIA, it’s a very clean decision. It makes sense. Both of them have cases. Does he want to deal with in his life what the reaction will be if he we

item.title: “I think Giannis is clearly hopeful that LeBron will …
item.description: Heat Central: “I think Giannis is clearly hopeful that LeBron will come and I think that was a good sign… If you’re LeBron you think it’s cool that Giannis is so wrapped up in what you might do and certainly the Heat are still absolutely on the board.” — @WindhorstESPN on LeBron to Miami rumors

item.title: The Lakers have been willing to trade Vanderbilt,
item.description: The Lakers have been willing to trade Vanderbilt, according to multiple sources who spoke with the Post who were granted anonymity so they could speak freely, as well as other players on the roster in order to create more roster-building optionality. The Cavaliers also have strong interest in Kuminga, a source said.

item.title: The Timberwolves are very much in the mix for LeBron …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "We are recording this on Tuesday evening, and so there is certainly any possibility that something happens tonight, tomorrow, whenever, to change things. But I would say that as things stand right now, one, it seems to be the perception around the league that LeBron is going to take his time with this decision. He is going to go through all of the options, talk things over with Rich Paul and his camp, and just really try to get a handle on what the best situation is for him. An

item.title: Teams 'skeptical' they can make a better case than Cleveland for LeBron James
item.description: Heat Central: Teams interested in LeBron James are skeptical that they can beat Cleveland’s case for LeBron, per @WindhorstESPN “What I suspect is going to be important to him is he is going to get through the regular season without having to go to extraordinary measures. Without having to play 38 minutes per game, take 25 shots. I think Philly can make a compelling case… but so can Cleveland. Can anybody make a more compelling case on the roster than Cleveland and more compelling on the home fr

item.title: Teams sending LeBron James voice notes to recruit him via Rich Paul
item.description: Heat Central: “Some of them have been able to send a voice note to LeBron James via Rich Paul…. It’s unclear whether Cleveland or Miami are among the teams that have sent a voice note to LeBron via Rich Paul. They kind of don’t need to, they already had him in their buildings” — @ShamsCharania on teams being able to send voice notes to recruit LeBron James

item.title: Bill Simmons on LeBron's free agency: 'The Cleveland thing is done'
item.description: Bill Simmons claimed that James going home to reunite with the Cleveland Cavaliers for the second time has already been determined. "The thing that happened to poor Golden State is, I think they thought they were getting LeBron and potentially [Anthony Davis] and now, it's pretty clear they were being used by leverage, as leverage, as LeBron goes back to Cleveland," the NBA pundit first said on the latest episode of his The Bill Simmons Podcast. When pressed by Joe House about the leverage he wa

item.title: The streets are talking and they're telling him that …
item.description: [ESPN Cleveland] Emmett Golden says The streets are talking and they're telling him that LeBron and Bronny are coming home: "Someone who reached out to me in the past and was right... reached out to me yesterday, late last night... Bron and Bronny back to Cleveland"

item.title: League executives who spoke with The Athletic on …
item.description: League executives who spoke with The Athletic on Thursday in Las Vegas at the official start of the NBA 2026 Summer League said there was only one team executive believed to have had direct contact with James during this free agency: Brandon Weems, a childhood friend to LeBron who works in Cleveland’s front office and appeared in photographs posted to social media of James and his friends partying in Akron, Ohio over the July 4 holiday.

item.title: (Veteran NBA reporter Chris Sheridan predicted on his …
item.description: The first, when he took his talents from Cleveland to South Beach in 2010, was unveiled in an ESPN TV special after weeks of in-person meetings with team executives. The second, when he returned to the Cavaliers in 2014, was revealed in an online open letter dictated by James to Sports Illustrated, after a private, face-to-face clearing of the air between James and Cavs owner Dan Gilbert. (Veteran NBA reporter Chris Sheridan predicted on his own website that James had decided to leave Miami for

item.title: Miami in the lead for LeBron James?
item.description: Heat Central: Sam Amick on LeBron to Miami rumors: “I had somebody tell me today that ‘I had one of the other teams tell me that they believe Miami is in the lead.’” He also said the Cavs seem optimistic and the Timberwolves are still hopeful.

item.title: "I'm hearing stuff in other cities too. I'm not gonna …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "I'm hearing stuff in other cities too. I'm not gonna say who but I had somebody on the phone with me today that I consider a good source who was swearing it's a done deal in another city," - @WindhorstESPN on the latest LeBron rumors 😯😯😯

item.title: "I have not heard that from a credible source,"
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "I have not heard that from a credible source," - @WindhorstESPN on if LeBron is trying to recruit Draymond Green to the Cavs.

item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “The only rightful farewell tour would be with the Cleveland Cavaliers,” - Charles Barkley on LeBron James.

item.title: [LeBron] is looking at Eastern Conference destinations …
item.description: Dru: “What I do believe, based on actual reporting is — Cleveland. Miami. Philly. [LeBron] is looking at Eastern Conference destinations with much more seriousness...He's apparently open to going very, very far away from where he's been for the last 8 years.” - @TheSteinLine

item.title: Windhorst: Cleveland is not the only place where there are LeBron rumors
item.description: While attempting to temper Cavaliers fans' expectations during a radio hit in Cleveland, ESPN's Brian Windhorst said he "had somebody on the phone with me today that I consider a good source who was swearing it's a done deal in another city [outside of Cleveland] a couple hours ago." "And so, I am being very, very cautious. Right now, it's too scattered," Windhorst said. "I also know how LeBron has operated. They have kept their circle very tight. ... I'm hearing rumors all day long, and all of

item.title: Brian Windhorst poured cold water Friday on …
item.description: ESPN’s Brian Windhorst poured cold water Friday on speculation that James is attempting to recruit longtime Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green away from the franchise, saying he has not heard anything to substantiate the claim. “I have not heard that from a credible source,” Windhorst said Friday on ESPN Cleveland. “If you’re asking me whether that was something that could happen, that’s a different conversation. But I have not heard that from a credible source.”

item.title: “Hey guys. I’ve got it on good authority that not only …
item.description: Jonathan Gotschall, the former Caroline’s Cupcakes owner whose bakery drew national attention in 2014 for calling James’ return to Cleveland, is now floating another wild rumor. In a post from POKÉ Fresh Ohio, which he now owns, Gotschall claimed James is not only coming back to Cleveland but also bringing son Bronny James and Draymond Green with him. “Hey guys. I’ve got it on good authority that not only is LeBron coming back to Cleveland, but he’s bringing Bronny and Draymond with him!” Gotsc

item.title: LeBron James to the Warriors still a possibility?
item.description: NBA Courtside: Jake Fischer on LeBron and the Warriors: “I do think the Warriors are still as involved as any team. I want to say that too. As much as the reunion with Cleveland and Miami would be storybook, this new all star team, traveling team USA kind of circus, high entertainment value thing that would exist is GS, is something that is still very much on the table.”

item.title: Cavs, Heat and Sixers frontrunners in LeBron James sweepstakes?
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on LeBron James: “Every team that’s in it still believes they have a chance and is still engaged with Rich Paul on a daily basis, hourly basis. Figuring out what it would look like, what the vision is. CLE, MIA, GSW, MIN, PHI, this are the 5 most teams that I’ve been hearing…. When I talk to other teams, they look at Cleveland a lot, Miami, and Philadelphia. Those are the 3 teams that others view as strong here.” (Via @SiriusXMNBA)

item.title: “I don’t think there’s a decision that’s been made but …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “I don’t think there’s a decision that’s been made but say if there was, it’ll make you think twice about it,” - Draymond Green on his pitch to LeBron James in free agency

item.title: “We’re in the mix for the greatest player of all time” …
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: “We’re in the mix for the greatest player of all time” Cavs Head Coach Kenny Atkinson discusses with @WorldWideWob, @DarthAmin, and Ryan McDonough what it would be like to have LeBron return to Cleveland.
